1428 S. MARENGO AVE., ALHAMBRA, CA 91803

2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 5.18 3.77 5.31 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.43 0.66 0.45 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.51 0.84 1.55 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.23 2.27 3.31 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.95 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.15 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.54
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.28
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.65
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
21.7%
annual
RN turnover
45.5%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.34
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.97
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-30 23 18 5 108 1 108
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-08 25 11 15 128 1 128
Total weighted health score
113.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
1
last cycle

Deficiencies (64)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
62 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2026-01-23 2026-02-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-12-23 2026-01-20
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-11-26 2025-12-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-08-28 2025-09-12
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-07-29 2025-08-20
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-21
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-18
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-10-21 2024-10-21
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2024-10-02 2024-10-21
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. B 2024-09-05 2024-09-26
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-09-05 2024-09-26
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-07-29 2024-08-16
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-07-29 2024-08-16
0573 Let each resident or the resident's legal representative access or purchase copies of all the resident's records. D 2024-06-27 2024-07-09
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-29
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-01-03 2024-02-02
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-11-22 2023-12-04
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-23
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-23
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. D 2023-07-26 2023-08-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-04-06 2023-05-03
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-02-10 2023-03-16
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2023-02-10 2023-03-16
